coredata.dc:title: Multi-time scale electric vehicle charging load forecasting considering constant current charging and parallel computing
coredata.prism:copyright: © 2022 The Author(s). Published by Elsevier Ltd.
coredata.prism:publisher: The Author(s). Published by Elsevier Ltd.
coredata.prism:issueName: 2022 The 5th International Conference on Electrical Engineering and Green Energy
coredata.dc:creator: Bian, Haihong
Guo, Zhengyang
Zhou, Chengang
Peng, Shan
coredata.dc:description: At present, the concept of ecological civilization has been widely recognized by the whole world, and a series of policies that guarantees the evolution of the electric vehicle (EV) industry has been implemented by multiple countries. Therefore, it is significant to predict the charging load of EVs to solve the challenges of power system planning and operation. The Monte Carlo (MC) method is preferred by many scholars in EV charging load prediction because it is very suitable for describing random characteristics with a good prediction effect. To obtain more reliable and efficient prediction results, this paper analyzes the application of parallel computing technology in MC simulation. Firstly, EVs in the region are classified according to their battery capacity. Based on the voltage change curve of lithium-ion power batteries in the process of constant current charging under different capacities, the charging power, charging time, and state of charge (SOC) of EVs are investigated. Secondly, the behavior characteristics of users and the driving parameter characteristics of EVs are studied respectively, and the probability distribution model of multi-source information is established. Thirdly, parallel computing technology in the computer field is introduced, and an improved MC method is proposed based on the multi-core CPU architecture. After fully considering the complex constant current charging process after fitting, the charging load of EVs in a region of East China is simulated. Finally, the time cost and the load forecasting results of serial and parallel methods are compared and analyzed, and the progressiveness and effectiveness of the parallel method are verified. Results show that the charging load has four peaks in a day, taxis are the main source of the peak load of the power grid, the charging load of buses fluctuates the most, and private cars are the main backup capacity to participate in V2G dispatching in the future. In addition, under the experimental conditions set in this paper, compared with the traditional serial MC method, the improved MC method based on parallel computing shows good performance with the acceleration effect improved by 7 to 12 times.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Load forecasting
Constant current charging process
Electric vehicle
Monte Carlo method
Parallel computing

xocs:item-toc-entry.xocs:item-toc-section-title: Analysis of factors influencing EV charging load characteristics
Charging characteristic curve during constant current charging
Analysis of users’ willingness to charge
Improved Monte Carlo method based on parallel computing
Improved Monte Carlo method based on CPU multi-core parallel computing
Validity verification and prediction result analysis of improved Monte Carlo method
Performance analysis of improved Monte Carlo method

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: (a) SOC–T characteristic curves of normal charging; (b) P–T characteristic curves of normal charging; (c) SOC–T characteristic curves of fast charging; (d) P–T characteristic curves of fast charging.
Charging characteristic curve of electric bus.
Flow chart of improved Monte Carlo method.
The number of different types of EVs.
Charging load prediction results of buses, taxis, and private cars.
(a) CPU Utilization; (b) The relationship between the number of cycles and time in the serial method; (c) The relationship between the number of cycles and time in the parallel method.
Running time of parallel and serial method.
Multiple times of serial and parallel computing.
Charging frequency of private car users.

row.entry: Normal distribution of initial charging time

N
( 4 . 2 , 0 . 1
8
2
)
(This refers to the lognormal distribution of the mileage of the buses after each charge)

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: The pseudo-code of the improved Monte Carlo method.

entry.bold: Improved Monte Carlo method based on parallel computing

row.entry:
initialize matrix Time_pri;// Time_pri is a matrix with N rows and 1440 columns, which stores the power information of each time period.
int pri;// pri is the number of corresponding private cars in a certain charging period.
start parallel for int i
≔ 1 to N

initialize S
≔ zeros(pri , 1); Time_Bus(i , :) ≔ 0;// S stores the SOC corresponding value of each private car.
generate d,c by Table 2;// d is the mileage between the completion of the last charging and the start of the next charging of each private car.
update S by d,c;// c is the energy consumption per 100 km of each private car.
for int j
≔ 1 to pri
if S(j , 1) >= r then g
≔ rand;// r and w are thresholds, which are determined by Table 1.
if g < w then update S(j , 1);
end if;
end if;
end for;
update tp,tr by S and (1);// tp is the corresponding charging time of each private car in the whole charging process.
Extract temporary travel probability and update tr;// tr is the remaining charging time of each private car.
for int j
≔ 1 to pri
if S(j , 1) < 0.7 then
while True
int T
≔ round(60 * normrnd);// T is the charging start time extracted according to the normal distribution in Table 2.
if T belongs to the corresponding period then
if T + tr(j , 1) <= 1440 then
for int t
≔
T to T + tr(j , 1)
Tv_T
≔
Time_pri(i , :);// If Tv is used as a prefix, it means that the variable is a temporary variable.
int Tv_t
≔
t - T + tp(j , 1); compute Tv_p by Tv_t and (1); Tv_T(t) ≔
Tv_T(t) + Tv_p; Time_pri(i , :) ≔
Tv_T(t);
end for;
break;
else if T + tr(j , 1) >= 1440 then
for int t
≔
T to 1440
Tv_T
≔
Time_pri(i , :);
int Tv_t
≔
t - T + tp(j , 1); compute Tv_p by Tv_t and (1); Tv_T(t) ≔
Tv_T(t) + Tv_p; Time_pri(i , :) ≔
Tv_T(t);
end for;
for int t
≔ 1 to T + tr(j , 1) - 1440
Tv_T
≔
Time_pri(i , :);
int Tv_t
≔ 1440 - T + t + tp(j , 1); compute Tv_p by Tv_t and (1); Tv_T(t) ≔
Tv_T(t) + Tv_p; Time_pri(i , :) ≔
Tv_T(t);
end for;
break;
end……;
end parallel for;

ce:dochead.ce:textfn: The 5th International Conference on Electrical Engineering and Green Energy, CEEGE 2022, 8–11 June, Berlin, Germany

head.ce:title: Multi-time scale electric vehicle charging load forecasting considering constant current charging and parallel computing

ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Nanjing Institute of Technology, No. 1, Hongjing Avenue, Jiangning Science Park, Nanjing, 211167, China

sa:affiliation.sa:address-line: No. 1, Hongjing Avenue, Jiangning Science Park

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: Nanjing Institute of Technology, No. 1, Hongjing Avenue, Jiangning Science Park, Nanjing, 211167, China

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: At present, the concept of ecological civilization has been widely recognized by the whole world, and a series of policies that guarantees the evolution of the electric vehicle (EV) industry has been implemented by multiple countries. Therefore, it is significant to predict the charging load of EVs to solve the challenges of power system planning and operation. The Monte Carlo (MC) method is preferred by many scholars in EV charging load prediction because it is very suitable for describing random characteristics with a good prediction effect. To obtain more reliable and efficient prediction results, this paper analyzes the application of parallel computing technology in MC simulation. Firstly, EVs in the region are classified according to their battery capacity. Based on the voltage change curve of lithium-ion power batteries in the process of constant current charging under different capacities, the charging power, charging time, and state of charge (SOC) of EVs are investigated. Secondly, the behavior characteristics of users and the driving parameter characteristics of EVs are studied respectively, and the probability distribution model of multi-source information is established. Thirdly, parallel computing technology in the computer field is introduced, and an improved MC method is proposed based on the multi-core CPU architecture. After fully considering the complex constant current charging process after fitting, the charging load of EVs in a region of East China is simulated. Finally, the time cost and the load forecasting results of serial and parallel methods are compared and analyzed, and the progressiveness and effectiveness of the parallel method are verified. Results show that the charging load has four peaks in a day, taxis are the main source of the peak load of the power grid, the charging load of buses fluctuates the most, and private cars are the main backup capacity to participate in V2G dispatching in the future. In addition, under the experimental conditions set in this paper, compared with the traditional serial MC method, the improved MC method based on parallel computing shows good performance with the acceleration effect improved by 7 to 12 times.

ce:data-availability.ce:para: The data that has been used is confidential.

ce:section.ce:para: The global average surface temperature will rise as greenhouse gas emissions continue to rise by the middle of the 21st century according to the Fifth Climate Change Report of the Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change (IPCC). The global average temperature will rise by 0.3 ∼ 4.8 °C by the end of the 21st century. Extreme climate events will increase significantly with the rise of global average surface temperature. Traditional fuel vehicles are one of the main sources of greenhouse gases, so it is urgent to promote the reform of the new energy vehicle industry. Compared with traditional fossil fuel vehicles, EVs have the advantages of environmental care, high energy conversion efficiency, and less noise. The automobile industry is evolving towards electrification, and countries all over the world are actively developing EVs. However, mass penetration of EVs is bound to destabilize the power grid with the strong intermittentness and randomness of EV charging load. Therefore, the charging load prediction of EVs is conducive to energy scheduling, so as to improve the economy and safety of power grid operation.

The MC method is based on probability and statistics theory, which is used to solve problems related to probability and random events. It is very applicable to construct the EV charging load model. Ref. [1] used the gray Verhulst model and Monte Carlo simulation method to predict the annual peak load curve and hourly load curve of typical days in different seasons of the target year. Ref. [2] analyzed the different tendencies of drivers with different social characteristics when choosing charging locations, and studied the effect of drivers’ gathering behavior on charging demand. Ref. [3] established a probabilistic model of the charging demand of EVs based on the stochastic travel chain and Markov Decision Process (MDP).

When a lot of computation is involved, the traditional serial method is unable to meet the time cost requirement. Based on the calculation model of weighted average filtering and the feature of the high-performing computer, Ref. [4] proposed a hierarchical image weighted average filtering parallel algorithm, which can fully express the parallelism of the model. Ref. [5] proposed a multi-core parallel particle swarm optimization (MPPSO) algorithm to increase the computing efficiency of hydropower systems running in the optimal state for a long time. A multi-group parallel computing strategy was proposed, and multiple groups were assigned to different CPU cores for parallel computing based on the dial-and-conquer strategy. The results calculated by different CPUs were combined to achieve a better acceleration effect and a more accurate optimal scheduling scheme. Ref. [6] conducted research based on continuous data in nanoscale cross arrays and proposed a scalable large-scale parallel computing scheme that can be applied to intelligent edge devices based on the idea of parallelization.

When using the traditional MC method to predict EV charging load, researchers often use the constant power charging model. However, most of the charging process of EV belongs to the constant current charging process, in which the charging voltage amplitude will change with the change of SOC, resulting in the change of actual charging power [7]. In order to accurately fit the voltage change of the power battery charging process, it needs to use extremely complex high-order functions. However, under the traditional serial MC method, the investment of large-scale complex high-order functions will lead to a huge increase in time overhead. In view of this situation, this paper proposes an improved MC simulation method based on CPU multi-core parallel computing. The total cycle is divided into several sub-cycles, and each core of the CPU processes sub-cycles independently. Finally, under the same external conditions, experiments are designed according to the data provided by a power company to compare the performance of the traditional serial MC method and the method proposed in this paper. The experimental results show that on the basis of ensuring the validity of the prediction results, the method achieves a speedup ratio of 4–13 times, which greatly improves the simulation efficiency. The higher the number of cycles, the more obvious the acceleration effect is. Under the condition of large-scale calculation, the running time of the proposed method is approximately linear with the number of cycles, showing good stability.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Analysis of factors influencing EV charging load characteristics
ce:section.ce:para: Taking a high-tech zone in East China as an example, EVs are mainly divided into three categories: Electric private cars, electric taxis, and electric buses. The battery capacities of typical electric private cars in this region are 80 kWh, 57 kWh, 40 kWh, and 30 kWh respectively. Electric taxis’ battery capacities are 57 kWh and 40 kWh. Considering battery aging, the battery capacity of most electric buses is 125 kWh, and the rest is regarded as 94.5 kWh. In addition, when charging piles supply power to EVs, electric energy loss will be generated [8], so the actual charging time will be affected.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Charging characteristic curve during constant current charging
ce:section.ce:para:
Constant current and constant voltage charging are mainly used in this area, that is, constant current charging and constant voltage charging are combined to charge the battery in the charging process. This mode effectively avoids the damage to the battery caused by the excessive initial current of constant voltage charging, not only solves the problems of a long time and low efficiency of small rate constant current charging, but also effectively avoids the battery temperature rise or overcharge in the later stage of large rate constant current charging, and reduces the damage to the battery. In the early stage of charging, the current is constant and the voltage rises gradually. When the SOC value reaches about 80%, it enters the constant voltage charging stage. At this stage, the voltage remains almost constant and the current decreases rapidly. The expressions of Power–Time (P–T) and SOC–Time (SOC–T) of each typical EV under constant current charging are obtained by fitting the battery data with polynomials:
(1)
f
( x )
=
p 1 ⋅
x
7
+ p 2 ⋅
x
6
+ p 3 ⋅
x
5
+ p 4 ⋅
x
4
+ p 5 ⋅
x
3
+ p 6 ⋅
x
2
+ p 7 x + p 8
p 9 ⋅ x + p 10

The charging power of normal charging is generally in the range of 5.4–7 kW, with a long charging time and low installation cost, which is conducive to extending battery life. In normal charging mode, the SOC–T and P–T curves of private cars and taxis in the constant current charging process are fitted, as shown in Fig. 1(a) and (b). The charging power of fast charging is generally in the range of 25–65 kW, with a short charging time and high installation cost, which has an adverse impact on the battery life. Therefore, when fast charging is adopted, when the SOC reaches 80%, the charging is considered to be stopped and the user will drive out of the charging station. In fast charging mode, the SOC–T and P–T curves of private cars and taxis in the constant current charging process are fitted, as shown in Fig. 1(c) and (d).

As an important means of urban transportation, buses need to carry a lot of public traffic flow in the city and usually operate in shifts. The SOC–T and P–T curves of electric buses in the constant current charging process are fitted, as shown in Fig. 2.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Analysis of users’ willingness to charge
ce:section.ce:para: The statistical results show that users tend to charge when the SOC is in the range of 50% to 70% [9]. Since the daily mileage of private cars is mostly within 100 km, most users will not charge every day, but connect to the power grid when the battery power is less than 60% to prolong the battery’s service life. According to the analysis results of users’ willingness to charge, this paper assumes that the daily power consumption of users has the following relationship with the charging frequency, as shown in Table 1.

It is pointed out in [10] that the travel mode of EV users and the accuracy of its probability distribution model are the key factors affecting the simulation and prediction of EV charging load. On weekdays, private cars often charge in residential parking lots after work or near their workplaces during working hours. In order to prolong the battery life, private car owners often use normal charging mode when charging at night. Electric buses often adopt shift operation system, and the driving mode is relatively fixed. In order to meet the normal operation needs, the electric bus often adopts the cluster fast charging mode, and its charging frequency is about 2 to 3 times a day. The daily mileage of taxis is around 300 km. Therefore, for most electric taxis, charging only once a day is unable to meet their normal power demand. Due to the large passenger flow, electric taxi owners need to supplement electric energy in a limited time, electric taxis usually choose the quick charging mode during the day, and adopt normal charging mode after passing the early morning passenger peak. In this paper, the 100 km energy consumption interval of each vehicle is determined by referring to the data provided by U.S. Environmental Protection Agency. Based on the NHTS2017 data set [11] and statistics provided by local power companies, the driving characteristics of users are shown in Table 2.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Improved Monte Carlo method based on parallel computing
ce:section.ce:para: The MC method is a method of using massive random data to simulate accurate logic and a calculation idea of using quantity to obtain quality. In order to obtain more reliable results, a large number of accurate calculations are inevitable, and the MC method has natural parallelism. The whole simulation space can be divided into multiple subspaces according to the number of cycles, which makes it possible to use parallel computing to carry out parallel simulation in multiple subspaces.

The MC method belongs to the category of probability analysis, which quantifies the uncertainty of variables through probability distribution and is suitable for describing the characteristics of things with randomness and the process of physical experiments. Due to the stochastic nature of drivers’ charging behavior characteristics, daily driving mileage, 100 km energy consumption, and other factors, the MC method can give full play to its inherent advantages in EV load forecasting.
The steps of EV charging load prediction based on the traditional serial MC method are as follows:

ce:list-item.ce:para: Calculate the current SOC of the EV and determine whether the user participates in the charging activity. If so, go to (5). If not, go to (8);
The initial charging time is randomly selected and the charging time is calculated;
Randomly select the time when the user stops charging, and calculate the charging power in the charging period;
The charging load in the charging period is superimposed on the basic load of the power grid in this period;
Calculate the average charging load of EVs and obtain the power demand curve.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Improved Monte Carlo method based on CPU multi-core parallel computing
ce:section.ce:para: Considering that there is no interdependence between the cycles of the MC method, which has the feasibility of parallelization, this paper evenly distributes the total number of cycles to each core of the CPU, so that each core of the CPU can execute the task of the corresponding sub-cycle independently. Under the environment of MATLAB R2018b, this paper completes the experiment based on the 11th Gen Intel(R) Core (TM) I7-11700 processor. This CPU has 8 cores, each core can be allocated about 8/N cycles when using parallel computing. Fig. 3 shows the detailed flow of the parallel algorithm optimization MC method to realize regional EV load forecasting.
The steps of the improved MC method based on parallel computing are as follows:

ce:list-item.ce:para: Determine the total number of cycles N;
Initializing related parameters in a parallel for loop;
The serial MC method is written into the parallel for loop as a sub-loop according to the rules of parallel computing;

Execute the parallel loop, and successively store the MC simulation results in the corresponding positions according to the processing speed of the multi-core pair sub-loop. When the total number of cycles reaches N, the parallel computing part ends.

ce:section.ce:para: In order to facilitate the demonstration, taking the load forecasting of electric private cars as an example, this paper gives the pseudo-code of the improved MC method based on parallel computing, as shown in Table 3.

Taking a region in East China as an example, the calculation example is set according to the data provided by a power company. After unified reduction, the number of three types of vehicles under typical capacity is rough as shown in Fig. 4. This chapter will conduct simulation experiments based on the above data, and summarize the experimental results.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Validity verification and prediction result analysis of improved Monte Carlo method
ce:section.ce:para: In this paper, the improved MC method based on parallel computing is used to simulate the regional EV charging load in working days for 10000 times. In order to verify the effectiveness of the method proposed in this paper, the traditional serial MC method is used to simulate under the same external conditions. The power demand prediction curves obtained by the two methods are shown in Figs. 5 and 6.

The simulation results show that the changing trend of the average power demand curve of the two methods is consistent, which proves the effectiveness of the proposed method. As can be seen from Fig. 5, in the disordered charging mode, there are four load peaks in a day. The peak section is concentrated around 4:00, 10:00, 14:30, and 22:00, and the load peak is particularly significant at 4:00 and 14:30, reaching about 4MW. As can be seen from Fig. 6, among the three types of cars, taxis have the highest charging demand and the peak load near 4:00 is caused by the charging behavior of taxis, and the peak load at 10:00, 14:30, and 22:00 are caused by the superposition of peak load of various vehicles. Due to the small number of buses and high charging power, the fluctuation of charging load is very high, and the difference between the upper and lower limits of power demand is obvious. The load peak near 10:00 is mainly caused by buses. Although private cars account for a large proportion of all cars, their low daily mileage indirectly affects their charging frequency, resulting in a low charging load.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Performance analysis of improved Monte Carlo method
ce:section.ce:para: This paper introduces the CPU utilization of the traditional serial MC method and the improved MC method based on parallel computing. As shown in Fig. 7(a), during the execution of two methods, the average utilization of CPU in serial and parallel computing per minute is 16% and 94% respectively. The experimental results show that compared with the traditional serial computing method, the parallel computing method makes full use of CPU computing resources and improves resource utilization by about 5 times.

This paper tests the simulation time of three types of electric vehicles under two methods, as shown in Fig. 8. The relationship between the number of cycles and the total running time of the two methods is shown in Fig. 7(b) and (c). The results show that with the increase in the number of cycles, the time consumed by the serial method will fluctuate greatly, which is mainly reflected in the simulation process of taxi charging load. However, the running time of the improved MC method proposed in this paper is approximately linear with the number of cycles.

Speedup ratio is the ratio of time consumed by the same task running in single processor system and parallel processor system. It is used to measure the performance and effect of parallel systems or program parallelization. Calculate the speedup ratio obtained by the improved MC method under different cycle times, as shown in Fig. 9. It can be seen that when the number of cycles is less than 100, the speedup ratio increases significantly with the increase of the number of cycles. When the number of cycles exceeds 100, the time overhead of parallel computing is about 1/8 of that of serial computing. When the number of cycles reaches more than 6000, the speedup ratio increases again and fluctuates around 12. Therefore, compared with the traditional serial method, the improved MC method proposed in this paper shows excellent performance, especially when it involves large-scale computing.

Based on the consideration of multi-source information, an improved Monte Carlo method based on parallel computing is proposed to predict the charging load of regional EVs. The following conclusions are obtained through example simulation:

ce:list-item.ce:para: On the basis of previous studies, the power change process of constant current charging of electric vehicles is considered. In order to improve charging efficiency and reduce battery loss, lithium battery charging optimization technology has been favored recently [12,13]. In the near future, multi-stage constant current charging and adaptive charging strategy will be widely used. Therefore, the study of power change during charging is helpful to improve the accuracy of load forecasting;

The disorderly charging behavior of taxis and buses will bring a large load to the power grid. The charging load of private cars is low and the number is large, which is a potential V2G dispatching target. In the follow-up research, reasonably guiding private cars to participate in V2G charging and discharging dispatching can help the power grid cut peaks, fill valleys, and enhance the stability of the power grid [14];

In the context of large-scale computing, the improved MC method using parallel computing technology can make full use of CPU resources and achieve a high speedup ratio. At present, the MC method is widely used in various disciplines [15,16]. Therefore, the method proposed in this paper has good application prospects.

ce:section.ce:para: Haihong Bian: Conceptualization, Supervision, Writing – review & editing, Project administration, Funding acquisition. Zhengyang Guo: Formal analysis, Parallel algorithm design, Writing – original draft, Writing – review & editing, Validation, Visualization. Chengang Zhou: Data curation, Investigation, Polynomial fitting of charging characteristic curve, Writing – review & editing. Shan Peng: Investigation, Writing – review & editing.

ce:conflict-of-interest.ce:para: The authors declare that they have no known competing financial interests or personal relationships that could have appeared to influence the work reported in this paper.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: This work is supported by the Jiangsu Province Postgraduate Practice Innovation Program, China : “Regional electric vehicle load forecasting based on parallel computing and multi-source information” (No. SJCX22_1079).

ce:bib-reference.ce:source-text: Sun Jinlei, Ma Qian, Liu Ruihang, et al. A novel multiobjective charging optimization method of power lithium-ion batteries based on charging time and temperature rise. Int J Energy Res 2019;43(13):7672-81.
